title: dianthus asper var. collinus
intro: Dianthus asper var. collinus, also known as the Rock Pink or Meadow Pink, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the carnation family. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of essential oils like borneol and camphor. The health benefits of Dianthus asper var. collinus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Dianthus asper var. collinus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
